title: All�s Well
content:
The clouds, which rise with thunder, slake
    Our thirsty souls with rain;
    The blow most dreaded falls to break
    From off our limbs a chain;
    And wrongs of man to man but make
    The love of God more plain.
    As through the shadowy lens of even
    The eye looks farthest into heaven
    On gleams of star and depths of blue
    The glaring sunshine never knew
meaning:
John Greenleaf Whittier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "All�s Well"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
